overview: The iShares Core Equity ETF Portfolio (XEQT.TO) is a 100% equity all-in-one ETF providing globally diversified stock exposure with no bonds. Similar to VEQT, it holds a mix of U.S., Canadian, international developed, and emerging market equities through underlying iShares index ETFs. XEQT automatically rebalances to maintain its target allocation. With an MER of 0.20%, it is slightly cheaper than VEQT (0.24%) and is the iShares alternative for investors who want maximum equity growth in a single ticker.
bullCase: 100% equity with global diversification at a competitive 0.20% MER.
Slightly cheaper than VEQT (0.20% vs 0.24%), saving costs over long holding periods.
All-in-one solution with automatic rebalancing — no portfolio management required.
bearCase: 100% equity means full drawdown exposure during market crashes.
No fixed-income allocation — not appropriate for conservative investors.
Currency exposure across multiple regions adds volatility for Canadian investors.
analystSummary: XEQT is the iShares answer to VEQT — 100% global equity at 0.20% MER. It's slightly cheaper and uses iShares underlying ETFs instead of Vanguard. Both are excellent all-equity options.
seoDescription: Explore XEQT ETF analysis: iShares Core Equity ETF Portfolio — 100% global equity at 0.20% MER. Should you buy XEQT?
